title: Podman version 4.2.1 in MacOS Error: missing command 'podman COMMAND
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Issue of MacOs, message Error: missing command 'podman COMMAND

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. delete ssh of macOs
2. uninstall podman with brew
3. install podman 

**Describe the results you received:**
Result:
When finish of installation, prints all normal message about podman use, but in de final line prints
`Error: missing command 'podman COMMAND'`

After if you run `podman ps`, podman response is

```
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: connection to bastion host (ssh://core@localhost:56601/run/user/1744420170/podman/podman.sock) failed: dial tcp [::1]:56601: connect: connection refused
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
When install podman the result expected is not receive error message in the final line
When run `podman ps` the result expected is the process of container and if there aren't containers, result just be title of description of process.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.2.1
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: connection to bastion host (ssh://core@localhost:56601/run/user/1744420170/podman/podman.sock) failed: dial tcp [::1]:56601: connect: connection refused
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
el paquete podman no está instalado ---> not found package podman 
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

comments.body:
How are you installing Podman Using Brew or the install link?
@ashley-cui @baude PTAL
Hi, @rhatdan yes, I runned `brew install podman`
It would seem that there is a path issue, but I am by far not a Mac expert.
Looks like your podman machine isn't up, have you done a `podman machine init` and a `podman machine start` ?
The error is also expected behavior, it's reminding you that you need a secondary command after `podman` ie, `podman run alpine` etc.

@ashley-cui i stoped and started podman vm 
steps:

1. `podman machine stop`
res: `Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully`

2. `podman machine start`
res:
```
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: dial unix /var/folders/wz/wvtknqz9249ct804ffv28bhslzlg__/T/podman/podman-machine-default_ready.sock: connect: no such file or directory
```
Try a `podman machine rm` and then a `podman machine init` again.
Now it reponses `Error: running vm "podman-machine-default" cannot be destroyed`
Please stop your machine before doing a rm.
I could run podman. Thank you!!
